title: S3 Events, Telemetry, Inventory, Capacity, and Cost · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A TypeScript pairing room incrementally builds observable storage and cost control model, validates one changed runtime input, and keeps earlier HTTP, accessibility, security, stream, AbortSignal, and resource-budget rules active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of observable storage and cost control model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, consume S3 notifications through supported destinations with duplicate, ordering, filtering, recursion, schema, delay, and lost-consumer recovery limits.

In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, correlate application operation, AWS request IDs, CloudTrail data events where justified, access logs, CloudFront logs, and stakeholder result without secrets.

In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, measure admitted and completed bytes, latency, failures, cache hit, range behavior, retries, backlog, replication, restore, and spend with bounded labels.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Event Delivery Semantics
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 4534 during a browser upload compatibility rollout. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to parse duplicate unordered events into bounded telemetry and cost decisions; separate deterministic browser evidence from controlled Node and AWS transfer gates; then abort during a stream or multipart promise and prove every task and part reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, consume S3 notifications through supported destinations with duplicate, ordering, filtering, recursion, schema, delay, and lost-consumer recovery limits.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Event Delivery Semantics.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for in a strict node 24 and typescript 7 service, consume s3 notifications through supported destinations with duplicate, ordering, filtering, recursion, schema, delay, and lost-consumer recovery limits. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Event Delivery Semantics, keep Event Delivery Semantics connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.

steps.instruction:
